name: Denny Price Family YMCA
description: Denny Price Family YMCA is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Enid, OK. There are 4 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A membership is required to play.

name: Government Springs Park South
description: Government Springs Park South is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Enid, OK. There are 8 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts are free. Players have access to restrooms. Reservations are not available.

name: Oakwood Activity Center
description: Oakwood Activity Center is a place to play pickleball in Enid, OK. There are 3 indoor wood and hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play. The courts can be reserved. Players have access to food, locker rooms, restrooms, water, and lights.
open_play_schedule: You can call and reserve a court during open gym floor hours.
fees: Fee required — $3 day pass to play unless you have an OAC membership. Membership includes pickleball.

name: Tea and Country Estate
description: Tea and Country Estate is a place to play pickleball in Enid, OK. There is 1 outdoor hard court. This is a dedicated court with a permanent net and lines. A one-time fee is required to play. Amenities include lights.
